data.sentence: Hi, this is Marcus Williams from Aegis Cloud Security support, am I speaking with Oliver Grant?
Yeah, that's me. Hi Marcus, thanks for getting back to me so quickly.
Of course, happy to help. So I'm pulling up support case number 3296 here — you're reporting a formatting issue with Aegis Comply v2 reports?
Yeah, exactly. So we just upgraded to — or I guess it launched yesterday, right, the v2 — and we ran our first on-demand SOC 2 report this morning and the output is just... it's not right.
Okay, can you describe what you're seeing? Like is it a visual formatting thing, or is data missing, or...?

It's — okay so a couple of things. First, the section headers in the PDF export are getting cut off. Like the text is truncating mid-word on some of the longer control category names. And then second, the control status table, the one that shows pass, fail, not applicable — it's rendering across two pages in a really awkward way. Like the header row is on one page and then all the data is on the next.
Got it, okay. And just to confirm — is this happening on every report you generate, or is it specific to the SOC 2 framework?
Uh, we only ran SOC 2 so far. We were planning to run a PCI DSS one later today but haven't done that yet.
Okay, that's helpful. And when you say PDF export — are you using the direct download from the Comply dashboard, or are you going through the API?
Dashboard. Just the download button in the UI.
Alright. And what browser are you using, just so I can note that down?
Chrome. Latest version, I keep it updated.

Okay, Chrome, noted. And um — can you send me a copy of the report that's showing the issue? You can attach it to the case, or if there's any sensitivity around that I can also try to reproduce it on my end.
Yeah, there's definitely sensitivity — it's a compliance report so I'd rather not send the whole thing externally. Can you reproduce it?
Absolutely, totally understand. Let me just spin up a test environment here and generate a SOC 2 report... give me just a second.
Sure, take your time.

Okay, so I'm generating it now... and yeah, I can actually see what you're describing. The header truncation is pretty noticeable on — looks like it's specifically the longer control category labels. Like the Availability and Confidentiality sections are fine but Processing Integrity is getting cut. And yeah the table pagination issue is there too.
Right, yeah, that's exactly it. Processing Integrity was one of the ones I noticed too.

Okay so this is definitely a bug on our end, I'm not going to ask you to clear your cache or anything like that — this is a rendering issue in the PDF generation service. Given that v2 just went GA yesterday this is almost certainly something that came in with the new release.
Yeah, that was my assumption too. Is this affecting other customers or is it just us?

I don't have visibility into other cases right now but I'm going to flag this to the engineering team immediately and check if there are other reports of the same thing. I'd expect this to be a known issue shortly if it isn't already.
Okay. And what's the timeline looking like for a fix? Because we have an internal audit review next week and we were hoping to have clean reports ready before that.

Yeah, I hear you, that's a real constraint. I can't give you a hard commitment on timeline right now without talking to engineering, but given that this is a rendering defect and not like a data integrity issue, these types of fixes tend to be relatively quick to patch. I'll escalate this as high priority and get back to you with an ETA today — is email okay for that?
Email works. And look, I appreciate that — I just want to make sure we're not scrambling the day before the review, you know.

Completely understand. I'll make sure the ticket reflects that timeline urgency. In the meantime — and this is just a workaround, not a solution — do you have access to the raw CSV export for the control status data? It won't have the same formatting obviously but at least the underlying data would be available if you need something to reference before the patch lands.
Hmm, yeah, I can dig into that. Where would I find that?

In the Comply dashboard, if you go to the report you generated, there should be an export dropdown — not just the PDF button — and there's a CSV option in there. It'll give you the full control list with statuses. It's not formatted for presentation but it has the data.
Okay, let me — yeah, I see it. Export as CSV. Alright, I'll grab that as a backup. That could work for our internal reference at least.

Good. And I'll also — let me just make sure I've got all this documented correctly in the case. So we've got: SOC 2 report, PDF export via the dashboard, section header text truncation on long control category names, and the control status table pagination breaking across pages. Anything else you noticed that I should add?
Uh... actually, yeah, there was one more thing. The page numbers in the footer — they seem off. Like page one of twelve or something, but the document is clearly more than twelve pages.
Oh interesting, okay. Let me check that on my end too... yeah, I see it — the page count in the footer isn't matching the actual document length. Okay, adding that to the bug report. Good catch.
Yeah, I figured it was probably all related but wanted to mention it.

Yep, likely all the same root cause in the PDF renderer. Okay, so next steps — I'm going to file this with engineering right after this call, flag the audit timeline urgency, and send you an email update today with whatever ETA they give me. Case number stays 3296, so you can reference that in any follow-up.
Sounds good. And if the fix does come through before next week, will we get notified automatically or do we need to re-download the report manually?

You'd need to regenerate the report — the system doesn't auto-regenerate existing ones. But once the patch is deployed I'll let you know via the case and you can just kick off a new on-demand report and it should render correctly.
Wait, sorry, that was me answering my own question — I meant to direct that to you, Marcus.
Ha, no worries — and actually you were right. Reports aren't stored and auto-updated, you'd generate a fresh one after the fix. It'll pull the latest data anyway so it'd be current.
Perfect. Okay, I think that covers it from my end. I appreciate you jumping on this quickly.
Of course. Sorry for the hassle on day two of the v2 launch — we'll get it sorted. You'll hear from me later today on the ETA. Have a good one, Oliver.
Thanks Marcus, you too.
